theme: microbes that feed on each other's waste
title: Waste-Fed Row: Cross-Feeding Biofilm Layout
strategies: naive monoculture: inoculate every cell with the same fixed species regardless of the instance (matches the evaluator's own weak reference, ratio ~0.1)
single most efficient consumer: read vmax off every species and fill the whole strip with the single highest-vmax species -- the tempting but self-poisoning trap
avoid-inhibition monoculture: fill the strip with a slower but uninhibited species to dodge self-poisoning -- still a monoculture, still leaves cross-feeding on the table

spatial cross-feeding layout: interleave the fast producer with its waste-consuming partner (chosen by comparing vmax*yield across all species matching its byproduct type) so waste never accumulates and both grow
